key_insight: HiMuon replaces one global Newton-Schulz update with independent tiled Newton-Schulz updates, cutting Muon’s dense-matrix update cost by localizing spectral interactions within tiles.
engineering_relevance: The method makes Muon-style optimization more GPU-friendly and scalable through small dense kernels, batching, chunking, and adaptable tile-size schedules.
method: Partition each momentum-gradient matrix into fixed-size T×T tiles.
Apply the same finite Newton-Schulz map separately to each tile.
Reassemble tile outputs, preserving local structure while discarding cross-tile coupling.
evidence_signal: The paper reports transformer-training experiments and controlled matrix-function diagnostics showing better optimizer-step efficiency while remaining close to full-matrix Muon in the tested regimes.
watch_item: Because HiMuon is a local matrix-function map rather than a convergent approximation to the full update, performance may depend strongly on tile size and may lose global spectral information.
paper_title: Hierarchical Muon: Tiled Newton-Schulz Updates for Efficient Muon Optimization
